description: Comprehensive dictionary of acronyms and abbreviations used in predictive maintenance scenarios and datasets
data_source: submission096 - PDMBench standardized predictive maintenance datasets

RUL.definition: The number of operational cycles or time units remaining before equipment failure
RUL.usage: Primary metric for predictive maintenance - predicts when equipment will fail

PHM.definition: A discipline focused on predicting when equipment failure might occur and managing health accordingly
PHM.usage: Broader field encompassing predictive maintenance, condition monitoring, and health assessment

PdM.definition: Maintenance strategy that uses data analysis to predict when equipment failure might occur
PdM.usage: Alternative to reactive (fix after failure) and preventive (fix on schedule) maintenance

MAE.definition: Average of absolute differences between predicted and actual values
MAE.usage: Metric for evaluating RUL prediction accuracy

RMSE.definition: Square root of the average of squared differences between predicted and actual values
RMSE.usage: Metric for evaluating RUL prediction accuracy, penalizes large errors more than MAE

CMAPSS.definition: NASA's turbofan engine degradation simulation dataset
CMAPSS.usage: Standard benchmark for RUL prediction in aerospace applications

CWRU.definition: Bearing fault dataset from CWRU's Bearing Data Center
CWRU.usage: Widely used for bearing fault classification and RUL prediction

FEMTO.definition: Bearing dataset from FEMTO-ST Institute, includes multiple operational scenarios
FEMTO.usage: Used for RUL prediction under varying operational conditions

IMS.definition: High-temporal-resolution bearing dataset from IMS Center, University of Cincinnati
IMS.usage: RUL prediction with high-frequency vibration data

HUST.full_form: Huazhong University of Science and Technology
HUST.definition: Bearing dataset with varying speeds and loads
HUST.usage: RUL prediction and fault classification under diverse operating conditions
HUST.characteristics: Multiple bearing types, varying speeds and loads

XJTU.definition: Bearing dataset with five different fault types
XJTU.usage: RUL prediction and fault classification with multiple fault modes

MFPT.usage: RUL prediction using frequency domain analysis
MFPT.characteristics: Very high sampling rate, suitable for frequency domain features

Mendeley.definition: Bearing dataset with non-stationary conditions (varying speed 600-1800RPM)
Mendeley.usage: RUL prediction under non-stationary signal conditions
Mendeley.characteristics: Non-stationary signals due to varying operational speed

Paderborn.definition: Bearing dataset with multimodal sensors (vibration, current, torque, speed)
Paderborn.usage: Fault classification and RUL prediction using multiple sensor modalities
Paderborn.characteristics: Multimodal sensors, artificial and natural degradation

GearboxUoC.definition: Gearbox fault dataset from University of Cincinnati

PlanetaryPdM.definition: Planetary gearbox dataset for component-specific fault localization
PlanetaryPdM.usage: Fault classification and component localization in planetary gearboxes

MAFAULDA.full_form: MAchine FAUlt Localization and Detection Algorithm

EngineMTQA.definition: Large-scale engine health question-answering dataset (110K+ questions)
EngineMTQA.usage: Testing LLM capabilities in understanding, perception, reasoning, and decision-making for engine health

HPC.definition: Component that compresses air at high pressure in a turbofan engine
HPC.usage: Common failure mode in CMAPSS dataset (HPC degradation)

LPC.definition: Component that compresses air at lower pressure in a turbofan engine
LPC.usage: Engine component monitored in EngineMTQA scenarios

HPT.definition: Turbine section operating at high pressure, extracts energy from high-pressure gas
HPT.usage: Engine component monitored in EngineMTQA scenarios

LPT.definition: Turbine section operating at lower pressure, extracts energy from low-pressure gas
LPT.usage: Engine component monitored in EngineMTQA scenarios

FD.definition: In CMAPSS context, refers to flight dataset number (FD001, FD002, etc.)

MLP.definition: Feedforward neural network with multiple layers
MLP.usage: Baseline model for RUL prediction and fault classification

LSTM.definition: Recurrent neural network architecture capable of learning long-term dependencies
LSTM.usage: Time series models for RUL prediction, handles sequential sensor data

CNN.definition: Deep learning architecture designed for processing grid-like data
CNN.usage: Fault classification from vibration signals, feature extraction

RNN.definition: Neural network architecture for sequential data

FAA.definition: US government agency responsible for aviation safety regulations
FAA.usage: Regulatory compliance for aircraft engine maintenance

EASA.definition: European agency responsible for aviation safety regulations
EASA.usage: Regulatory compliance for aircraft engine maintenance in Europe

ISO.usage: Vibration and mechanical standards for equipment health assessment

ANSI.usage: Vibration standards for bearing health assessment

IEC.definition: International standards organization for electrical and electronic technologies
IEC.usage: Standards for electric motor health assessment

NEMA.definition: US trade association for electrical equipment manufacturers
NEMA.usage: Standards for electric motor health assessment

AGMA.definition: US trade association for gear manufacturers

IEEE.full_form: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
IEEE.definition: Professional organization for electrical and electronics engineering
IEEE.usage: Standards for motor testing and health assessment

JSON.usage: Format for scenario definitions and agent outputs

API.definition: Set of protocols and tools for building software applications
API.usage: Interface for accessing tools and services in the agent framework

SME.definition: Person with deep knowledge and expertise in a specific domain
SME.usage: Experts who validate scenarios and provide domain knowledge

QA.definition: Natural language processing task of answering questions
QA.usage: EngineMTQA dataset format - questions about engine health

KDD.definition: ACM SIGKDD Conference on Knowledge Discovery and Data Mining
KDD.usage: Target conference for research paper submission

MCP.definition: Protocol for connecting AI applications to data sources and tools
MCP.usage: Referenced in MCP-Bench and MCP Universe papers

MLE.definition: Field combining machine learning with software engineering
MLE.usage: Referenced in MLE Bench paper - baseline for scenario definition

ITFormer.definition: Framework combining time series encoders with frozen LLMs for multi-modal QA
ITFormer.usage: Related work that created EngineMTQA dataset (110K questions)

PHMBench.definition: Domain-specific benchmarking framework for evaluating large models in PHM
PHMBench.usage: Related work - created 100 industry-specific scenarios for RUL prediction

PDMBench.definition: Standardized benchmark dataset for predictive maintenance (14 datasets)
PDMBench.usage: Source of datasets in submission096 directory

Azure.definition: Simulated aircraft engine degradation dataset from Microsoft Azure
Azure.usage: RUL prediction for aircraft engines under various operational conditions
Azure.characteristics: Simulated sensor data, multiple operational settings

ElectricMotorVibrations.definition: Vibration dataset from electric motors under various fault conditions
ElectricMotorVibrations.usage: Fault classification for electric motor bearing and rotor faults
ElectricMotorVibrations.characteristics: Acceleration measurements (AccX, AccY, AccZ), torque, ohm readings

RotorBrokenBar.definition: Dataset for detecting broken rotor bars in induction motors
RotorBrokenBar.usage: Fault classification for rotor bar failures in electric motors

CSV.definition: File format for storing tabular data
CSV.usage: Format for most dataset files (e.g., CWRU_train.csv)

TXT.usage: Format for CMAPSS datasets (train_FD001.txt, test_FD001.txt, RUL_FD001.txt)

JSONL.definition: File format with one JSON object per line

PSI.usage: Pressure measurements in equipment (e.g., gearbox oil pressure)

RPM.usage: Rotational speed measurements (e.g., bearing speed 600-1800 RPM)

KHz.usage: Sampling rate measurements (e.g., 97.656 KHz sampling rate)

F1.definition: Harmonic mean of precision and recall

Precision.definition: Ratio of true positives to all predicted positives
Precision.usage: Metric for fault classification accuracy per class

Recall.definition: Ratio of true positives to all actual positives
Recall.usage: Metric for fault classification completeness per class

Accuracy.definition: Ratio of correct predictions to total predictions
Accuracy.usage: Overall metric for fault classification performance

usage_notes.dataset_naming: Dataset names in scenarios should exactly match file names in submission096 directory (e.g., 'CMAPSS_FD001' matches 'train_FD001.txt')
usage_notes.acronym_variations: Some acronyms may have variations (e.g., 'UoC' vs 'GearboxUoC' for University of Cincinnati gearbox dataset)
usage_notes.case_sensitivity: Acronyms are typically case-sensitive in code but may vary in documentation
usage_notes.context_dependent: Some acronyms have multiple meanings depending on context (e.g., 'API' can mean different things in different domains)
